domain: Hopi snake dance three-role grasp protocol (Pueblo Southwest; rattlesnake handling via specialized trio: carrier holds in mouth, hugger keeps from coiling, gatherer retrieves dropped snakes; strict role boundaries; complementary safety checks)
specific_mechanism: 3-role specialisation with strict boundaries and complementary safety functions for handling high-risk live objects; each role has dedicated constraints + cannot perform other roles.
llm_application: SNAKE-DANCE-PROTOCOL: a 3-role agentic-LLM tool-call protocol for safety-critical actions. For each high-risk tool call (file write, irreversible API call, large transactions): (1) EXECUTOR sub-agent proposes the tool-call request; (2) CONSTRAINER sub-agent enforces argument-space safety policy PRE-call (can VETO or REWRITE arguments but NOT execute); (3) CONTAINMENT sub-agent runs POST-call to detect side-effect drift and trigger automatic rollback (cannot pre-empt). Strict role boundaries: Executor cannot block, Constrainer cannot execute, Containment cannot pre-empt. Temporal sequencing pre→in→post. Differs from generic multi-agent debate (Critic/Defender/Judge all argue) by ROLE-SPECIALISATION + STRICT-BOUNDARY + TEMPORAL-SEQUENCING for tool-call lifecycle.
novelty_claim: Multi-Agent Debate (Critic-Defender-Judge) is OPINION-LEVEL debate. MAVEN, VeriMAP, MAS-ProVe use verification gates but generic. AgentSpec/Pro2Guard use runtime enforcement (single-role). SNAKE-DANCE-PROTOCOL differs by (a) THREE ROLES SPECIALISED, (b) STRICT BOUNDARIES (each role cannot perform others), (c) TEMPORAL SEQUENCING (pre-call constrainer, in-call executor, post-call containment), (d) TOOL-CALL LIFECYCLE specifically. The trio + boundary + sequence combination is novel.
content_words: pre-call constraint + in-call execute + post-call rollback

llm_side: pre-call constraint + in-call execute + post-call rollback
